instructions:
You are an expert software engineer debugging C/C++ programs. You
interact with a Unix shell to investigate bugs and produce diagnoses.

queries.user_text:
You're debugging a bug in `bc-heap-overflow`, a real C program from the BugBench benchmark suite.

bc-1.06 heap buffer overflow in storage.c more_arrays(). The initialization loop on line 176 uses 'v_count' (variable count) as the upper bound instead of 'a_count' (array count). When v_count > a_count, the loop writes past the allocated a_names/arrays heap buffers, corrupting heap metadata and causing a crash in subsequent lookups.

You are in the project's source directory. The buggy binary is at `./bc_buggy`.
Key source file(s): bc/storage.c, bc/util.c, bc/bc.c.


The binary was compiled with AddressSanitizer (-fsanitize=address). Running it with the crashing input will produce an ASan report showing the exact overflow location.

Use bash to investigate: read the source, reproduce the crash, run gdb in batch mode, etc. Identify the root cause and propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.
queries.prompt:
You're debugging a bug in `bc-heap-overflow`, a real C program from the BugBench benchmark suite.

bc-1.06 heap buffer overflow in storage.c more_arrays(). The initialization loop on line 176 uses 'v_count' (variable count) as the upper bound instead of 'a_count' (array count). When v_count > a_count, the loop writes past the allocated a_names/arrays heap buffers, corrupting heap metadata and causing a crash in subsequent lookups.

You are in the project's source directory. The buggy binary is at `./bc_buggy`.
Key source file(s): bc/storage.c, bc/util.c, bc/bc.c.


The binary was compiled with AddressSanitizer (-fsanitize=address). Running it with the crashing input will produce an ASan report showing the exact overflow location.

Use bash to investigate: read the source, reproduce the crash, run gdb in batch mode, etc. Identify the root cause and propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.

tool_calls.call: pwd && ls -la && find . -maxdepth 3 -type f | sed 's#^./##' | sort | head -200
nl -ba bc/storage.c | sed -n '1,230p' && printf '\n--- util growth sites ---\n' && nl -ba bc/util.c | sed -n '540,650p'

printf '--- reproduce_crash.bc ---
'; cat -vet reproduce_crash.bc; printf '
--- input.bc head ---
'; head -80 input.bc | cat -vet; printf '
--- run reproduce ---
'; ASAN_OPTIONS=abort_on_error=0:detect_leaks=0 ./bc_buggy reproduce_crash.bc </dev/null; printf 'exit=%s
' $?; printf '
--- run input.bc ---
'; ASAN_OPTIONS=abort_on_error=0:detect_leaks=0 ./bc_buggy input.bc </dev/null; printf 'exit=%s
' $?

python3 - <<'PY' > /tmp/bc_overflow.bc
for i in range(1, 70):
    print(f'v{i} = 1')
for i in range(1, 40):
    print(f'a{i}[1] = 1')
print('a1[1]')
PY
printf '%s\n' '--- generated head/tail ---'
(head -5 /tmp/bc_overflow.bc; echo '...'; tail -8 /tmp/bc_overflow.bc) | cat -vet
printf '%s\n' '--- run generated ---'
ASAN_OPTIONS=abort_on_error=0:detect_leaks=0 ./bc_buggy /tmp/bc_overflow.bc </dev/null; printf 'exit=%s\n' $?

printf '%s\n' '--- try nonhalting ASan ---'; ASAN_OPTIONS=halt_on_error=0:abort_on_error=0:detect_leaks=0 ./bc_buggy /tmp/bc_overflow.bc </dev/null 2>&1 | head -220; printf 'pipeline_exit=%s\n' ${PIPESTATUS[0]}

cat > /tmp/gdbcmds <<'GDB'
set pagination off
set environment ASAN_OPTIONS abort_on_error=0:detect_leaks=0
break bc/storage.c:153
commands
silent
printf "more_arrays entry: v_count=%d a_count=%d next_array=%d next_var=%d\n", v_count, a_count, next_array, next_var
continue
end
run /tmp/bc_overflow.bc
bt
GDB
gdb -q -batch -x /tmp/gdbcmds --args ./bc_buggy 2>&1 | head -200

nl -ba bc/global.h | sed -n '80,155p'; printf '
--- init_tree and lookup init ---
'; nl -ba bc/util.c | sed -n '500,625p'; printf '
--- array storage users ---
'; nl -ba bc/storage.c | sed -n '340,390p;850,875p;920,935p'
grep -R "a_names\|f_names\|v_names" -n bc | head -200; printf '\n--- generated around boundary ---\n'; nl -ba /tmp/bc_overflow.bc | sed -n '60,110p'

cp bc/storage.c /tmp/storage.c.fixed && python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('/tmp/storage.c.fixed')
s=p.read_text()
s=s.replace('for (; indx < v_count; indx++)\n    arrays[indx] = NULL;', 'for (; indx < a_count; indx++)\n    arrays[indx] = NULL;')
p.write_text(s)
PY
diff -u bc/storage.c /tmp/storage.c.fixed | sed -n '1,80p'
printf '\n--- lookup code demonstrates pre-grow write too ---\n'
nl -ba bc/util.c | sed -n '569,583p'
